title: The freedom you paid for
claim: Category labels and headline yields sell stories. Holdings, structure, and planning pillars decide what you actually own.

blocks.title: Label vs holdings
blocks.body: Flexi-cap freedom is real only when the book uses it. Large-cap-heavy ‘go anywhere’ funds teach style drift — know the composition, not just the SEBI bucket.

blocks.title: Rules rename; conviction decides
blocks.body: Floors on multi-cap and the birth of flexi-cap show how regulation can fix labels without forcing managers to wander. Freedom unused is still a style.

blocks.title: Act or leave alone
blocks.body: When a flexi drifts, the hard judgment is tax, conviction, and whether selling fixes the mismatch you bought.

blocks.title: Four pillars before fund picks
blocks.body: Protection, growth, liquidity, and named goals. SIPs alone are not a plan. Inventory gaps honestly — self-ratings are inventory, not mastery.

blocks.title: Active share ≠ alpha
blocks.body: Construct a toy book until active share sits in a deliberate band. Difference from the index measures honesty of active risk — not whether that risk pays.

blocks.title: Yield under the lid
blocks.body: InvIT headlines can look like easy income. Separate seduction copy from structure: capital return, fees, contracts, unit price.

blocks.title: Crowded corners
blocks.body: Many folios can still be one crowded bet. Discriminate overlap from true spread before you call a shelf diversified.
